summary: R2 response to systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES for M4-GRAIN-SUPPLY-001. The implementation now emits explicit auditable grain supply events for reserve, consume, and release; reserve/release return M2 population-group deltas for actual grain stock changes; consumption accounts for normal consumption plus loss and records unmet carried-grain demand as shortage; loss-only exhaustion transitions reservations out of reserved state; and runtime validation rejects malformed duplicate GrainSupplyReservationId rows that span multiple campaign plans. Scope remains limited to M4 campaign grain reservations, release, consumption accounting, and forecast query.

tests_run.result: 1 test file passed; 7 tests passed. Added R2 coverage for reserve/consume/release events, M2 deltas, loss-only consumption, loss overflow converted to shortage, stable shortage reason ordering, no resource creation, and malformed duplicate reservation IDs across campaigns.
17 test files passed; 120 tests passed. Includes tests/sim-core-m4-grain-supply.test.ts.
1 test file passed; 8 tests passed. Protocol command/query schema coverage still passes for M4 grain reserve, consume, release, preview, and invalid loss reason.

tests_run.result: M4-GRAIN-SUPPLY-001 JSON parse/shape ok; status REVIEW and route_to systems_architect.

decisions: R3 risk remains acknowledged: protocol and WorldState surfaces are changed, so this R2 handoff routes back to systems_architect.
Reserve, consume, and release command results now carry explicit grain supply DomainEventV1 entries with command, actor, reservation, campaign, accounting, and revision audit fields.
Reserve/release M2 grain stock changes are reported through state.m2-population-group-updated deltas; release filters out zero-carried source rows so only actual stock changes are emitted.
Consumption applies normal consumption first, then loss, and any unmet remaining consumption or loss is recorded as shortage on a deterministic final source row rather than being dropped.
Loss-only consumption that exhausts carried grain now marks affected supply rows consumed instead of leaving them reserved.
Runtime validation enforces one CampaignPlanId scope per GrainSupplyReservationId, and consume/release reject malformed cross-campaign reservation IDs before authority-sensitive state changes.
risks: This remains an R3 protocol and runtime-state extension; systems_architect should review event shape and save/load event-tail compatibility before acceptance.
Save-format milestone work remains intentionally out of scope; only existing command/event-tail parsing was extended where sim-core already persists command/query tails.

Forecast remains intentionally bounded to M4 grain needs, existing reservations, controlled M2 stockpile availability, and seasonal risk. It does not model route capacity, naval logistics, market economy, march execution, battle, siege, UI, server, Rust/WASM, or production dependency behavior.
requested_action: R2 review by systems_architect 019f0188-b041-7d10-a689-85f3283e4013. Verify the requested changes are satisfied for explicit events/deltas, grain conservation and loss/shortage accounting, and GrainSupplyReservationId scope validation. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK; do not mark ACCEPTED or CLOSED from this writer handoff alone.
